definitions.description: Starts the workflow when a 1Shot API webhook is received and authenticated

webhooks.name: default

executionsHelp.inactive:
Webhooks have two modes: test and production.

Use test mode while you build your workflow. Click the 'listen' button, then make a request to the test URL. The executions will show up in the editor.

Use production mode to run your workflow automatically. Activate the workflow, then make requests to the production URL. These executions will show up in the executions list, but not in the editor.
executionsHelp.active:
Webhooks have two modes: test and production.

Use test mode while you build your workflow. Click the 'listen' button, then make a request to the test URL. The executions will show up in the editor.

Use production mode to run your workflow automatically. Since the workflow is activated, you can make requests to the production URL. These executions will show up in the executions list, but not in the editor.

triggerPanel.activationHint: Once you've finished building your workflow, run it without having to click this button by using the production webhook URL.

properties.name: webhookType

options.name: 1Shot Signature Verification
options.description: Standard 1Shot webhook with ED-25519 signature verification

options.name: X402 Payment Gateway
options.description: X402 payment-gated webhook requiring authorization

properties.description: Choose the type of webhook verification to use

properties.name: publicKey
properties.description: The ED-25519 public key provided by 1Shot for webhook verification

properties.name: multipleMethods
properties.description: Whether to allow the webhook to listen for multiple HTTP methods

properties.name: httpMethod
properties.description: The HTTP method to listen to

properties.name: httpMethod
properties.description: The HTTP methods to listen to

properties.name: path
properties.description: The path to listen to, dynamic values could be specified by using ':', e.g. 'your-path/:dynamic-value'. If dynamic values are set 'webhookId' would be prepended to path.

properties.name: responseMode

options.name: Immediately
options.description: As soon as this node executes

options.name: When Last Node Finishes
options.description: Returns data of the last-executed node

options.name: Streaming
options.description: Returns data in real time from streaming enabled nodes

properties.description: When and how to respond to the webhook

properties.displayName: Insert a node that supports streaming (e.g. 'AI Agent') and enable streaming to stream directly to the response while the workflow is executed. More details
properties.name: webhookStreamingNotice
responseCode

options.description: OK - Request has succeeded
Created - Request has been fulfilled
No Content - Request processed, no content returned
Moved Permanently - Requested resource moved permanently
Found - Requested resource moved temporarily
Not Modified - Resource has not been modified
Bad Request - Request could not be understood
Unauthorized - Request requires user authentication
Forbidden - Server understood, but refuses to fulfill
Not Found - Server has not found a match

options.name: Custom Code
options.description: Write any HTTP code

properties.description: The HTTP response code to return

properties.name: responseData

options.name: All Entries
options.description: Returns all the entries of the last node. Always returns an array.

options.name: First Entry JSON
options.description: Returns the JSON data of the first entry of the last node. Always returns a JSON object.

options.name: First Entry Binary
options.description: Returns the binary data of the first entry of the last node. Always returns a binary file.

options.name: No Response Body
options.description: Returns without a body

properties.description: What data should be returned. If it should return all items as an array or only the first item as object.

properties.displayName: If you are sending back a response, add a "Content-Type" response header with the appropriate value to avoid unexpected behavior
properties.name: contentTypeNotice
tokens
properties.description: The tokens that will be accepted for payment

values.name: paymentToken
values.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression

values.name: payToAddress
values.description: The address that will receive the payment. Should be in the form of an EVM address with the leading 0x.

values.name: paymentAmount
values.description: The minimum payment amount required to trigger the workflow. This is in Wei for the token specified. For example, USDC has 6 decimals, so for $1.00 payment enter 1000000.

options.name: allowedOrigins
options.description: Comma-separated list of URLs allowed for cross-origin non-preflight requests. Use * (default) to allow all origins.

options.name: resourceDescription
options.description: A description of this x402-gated resource

options.name: mimeType
options.description: The mime type of the resource. Leave blank for no mime type. For n8n, this is almost always application/JSON

options.name: ignoreBots
options.description: Whether to ignore requests from bots like link previewers and web crawlers

options.name: ipWhitelist
options.description: Comma-separated list of allowed IP addresses. Leave empty to allow all IPs.

options.name: noResponseBody
options.description: Whether to send any body in the response

options.name: rawBody
options.description: Whether to return the raw body

options.name: responseData
options.description: Custom response data to send

options.name: responseContentType
options.description: Set a custom content-type to return if another one as the "application/json" should be returned

options.name: responseHeaders
options.description: Add headers to the webhook response

options.name: responsePropertyName
options.description: Name of the property to return the data of instead of the whole JSON

options.name: x402RefundsContactEmail
options.description: Enter the email address to notify to adjudicate refunds using x402Refund.com

definitions.name: n8n-nodes-1shot.oneShotWebhook
